name: code2vision
description: Gives Claude Code visual access to your browser render and console for automated pixel-perfect QA

code2vision.devServerUrl.description: URL of your running dev server

code2vision.screenshotDelay.description: Milliseconds to wait after reload before taking a screenshot

code2vision.maxQAIterations.description: Maximum QA loop iterations before requesting human intervention

walkthroughs.title: Get started with code2vision
walkthroughs.description: Give Claude Code visual access to your browser for pixel-perfect QA.

steps.title: Install the browser engine
steps.description:
code2vision uses Playwright to capture screenshots and console logs. Install the Chromium browser engine to get started.

[Install Playwright Chromium](command:code2vision.installPlaywright)

steps.title: Open your project & start the dev server
steps.description:
Open your frontend project in VS Code and start your dev server.

```
npm run dev
```

By default code2vision looks for **http://localhost:5173**. You can change this in Settings → code2vision.devServerUrl.

steps.title: Connect Claude Code (add .mcp.json)
steps.description:
This adds code2vision as an MCP server so Claude Code can call `take_screenshot()`, `get_console_logs()` and more.

[Setup .mcp.json](command:code2vision.setupMcp)

Then **restart Claude Code** in your project folder.

steps.title: Open the Preview Panel
steps.description:
Open the code2vision panel to see your app render and console logs live.

[Open Preview Panel](command:code2vision.openPreview)

steps.title: Start the QA loop with Claude Code
steps.description:
Run Claude Code in your project and give it a prompt like:

```
Use take_screenshot() to see the current render,
then implement [your feature] and keep capturing
until it matches the design.
```

Claude will iterate automatically — editing code, waiting for hot-reload, and capturing screenshots until pixel-perfect.

scripts.build: npm run build:extension && npm run build:webview
scripts.watch: npm run watch:extension & npm run watch:webview
